title: The Adventures of Tom Sawyer, Complete
author: Twain, Mark
summary: The Adventures of Tom Sawyer follows mischievous Tom, a clever Missouri boy, through pranks, friendships, moral trials, and escapades along a Mississippi-side village. Raised by Aunt Polly, Tom dupes adults, whitewashes fences, joins pretend pirate bands, witnesses a graveyard murder, and wrestles with fear, conscience, and loyalty. He befriends Huck Finn, courts Becky Thatcher, faces Injun Joe’s menace, helps exonerate an innocent man, and explores haunted houses and caves. Play, danger, superstition, and youthful bravado yield moments of courage, guilt, and redemption as Tom matures while preserving boyish imagination and the river-town’s vivid social life, and small-town humor and nostalgia.

characters.name: Tom Sawyer
characters.lifespan: survives the adventures; matures somewhat by story's end

characters.name: Aunt Polly
characters.lifespan: remains guardian of Tom throughout the story

characters.name: Sid (Sidney)
characters.lifespan: continues at home; foil to Tom

characters.name: Jim (the small colored boy)
characters.lifespan: serves Tom and family; remains in household duties

characters.name: Becky Thatcher
characters.lifespan: courtship with Tom; remains part of Tom’s social circle

characters.name: Injun Joe
characters.lifespan: pursued; fate resolved in story (see chapters)

characters.name: Muff Potter
characters.lifespan: initially suspected of murder; later saved/exonerated

characters.name: Joe Harper
characters.lifespan: joins Tom’s adventures; safe return with others

characters.name: Widow Douglas
characters.lifespan: offers shelter; interacts with Huck and others

characters.name: Mr. Jones
characters.lifespan: appears in chapters concerning social events
